expectations.text: Reads .project/PROJECT_STATE.md (and ISSUES.md) before responding, rather than asking the user to explain the project first
expectations.evidence: Narration steps 2-4: read PROJECT_STATE.md/.json and ISSUES.md/.json before producing any response.

expectations.text: Immediately presents a Resume Brief as its first substantive response
expectations.evidence: Final message opens with '## Resuming: Customer Support RAG Agent' in the skill's exact template, with no clarifying question asked of the user first.

expectations.text: The resume brief correctly states the last completed phase (Phase 3) and next phase (Phase 4 - Retrieval Pipeline)
expectations.evidence: 'Last completed (per docs): Phase 3 -- Embedding & Vector Store', 'Next up (per plan): Phase 4 -- Retrieval Pipeline'.

expectations.text: The resume brief correctly states overall progress as 3 of 9 phases complete
Surfaces the one open issue (ISSUE-001, PDF chunking losing section headers) in or immediately after the brief
expectations.evidence: 'Open issues: 2 -- ISSUE-001 (Warning: PDF chunking drops section headers on long policy docs), ISSUE-002 (Blocker, new...)'.

expectations.text: Ends by asking whether to continue with the next phase or review the open issue first, and pauses
expectations.evidence: Final line: 'Ready to continue with Phase 4, or want to look at the open issues first? Specifically for ISSUE-002...' -- then stops.

eval_feedback.overall: This run also went beyond the assertions in a way worth highlighting: it caught that the working directory's actual files don't match what PHASE_LOG.md claims was built (an artifact of this eval's fixture only seeding .project/ docs, not matching stub source), and per the skill's 'code is ground truth, not docs' + 'flag it, don't silently route around it' rules, logged a proper ISSUE-002 (Blocker) using the full issue template and kept PROJECT_STATE.md's open-issues list and session history in sync. None of the current assertions test this formal-issue-logging behavior explicitly -- worth adding one, since it's a concrete, structural difference from the baseline's ad hoc handling of the same discovery (see without_skill grading notes).
